channel.title: Comments for page "Deer And The Overseers"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Deer And The Overseers"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite enjoyed the student's perspective on the Foundation and the interesting history of interactions between them and Deer.

I agree with ARD's concerns about information security, but it didn't dissuade me from giving a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, that's exactly what it is. Sounds like I got the tone right, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this reads -exactly- like the sort of self-congratulatory pap that gets published in student magazines when they fight the man. I can't tell if I love it or hate it for that, null-vote :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With regards to the second one — O5-4 isn't publicly known as O5-4. The world only knows him as Jean Lemieux Betrand. The Insurgency only knows who he is because they've spent years tracking this information down. There's no conceivable reason for him to openly proclaim that he's the Fourth Overseer; that's just a huge information breach which paints a target on his back.

Well, that's just justification for him going. In the context of the speech, he still kept his identity secret when he attended - they didn't know his name, maybe only his face.

Fair enough for the other two, but learning about one was the point.

And with regards to the third — why couldn't a member of Red Right Hand thaumaturgically disguise themselves as a teenager or even just an adult looking to go to college late?

Eh, that seems more complicated than the one who does it himself and seems involved in fieldwork already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm willing to give O5-10 a pass, but that doesn't answer how anyone at Deer would learn that the army is connected to O5-5 or how O5-4's identity would become public.

With regards to the second one — O5-4 isn't publicly known as O5-4. The world only knows him as Jean Lemieux Betrand. The Insurgency only knows who he is because they've spent years tracking this information down. There's no conceivable reason for him to openly proclaim that he's the Fourth Overseer; that's just a huge information breach which paints a target on his back.

And with regards to the third — why couldn't a member of Red Right Hand thaumaturgically disguise themselves as a teenager or even just an adult looking to go to college late?

Even within the context of TWIE, most of my concerns remain unanswered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The majority of these issues are because of the characterization of the Overseers in the source material that this draws on: djkaktus's Proposal III. This is a pretty well-known piece and is linked at the start of this article.

  1. Some of the events and specific Overseer Deaths are incredibly high-profile. The Tenth Overseer's death in that story would be public to anyone in the Wanderer's Library (which Deer College is established as having relations with). The Fifth Overseer deployed 10,000 men to try and catch the Insurgents. The Fourth Overseer died in an extremely public terrorist attack by Samsara on an airport. Anyone of these events could theoretically be learnable by Deeries.
  2. In The Way It Ends, the Fourth Overseer takes on a highly active and almost public role. The Insurgents find out that he canceled an appearance over normal civilian television broadcast. If he's appearing at nonanomalous speaking engagements, why would a potential recruitment bid be unusual? The President and Vice President frequently make speaking engagements.
  3. My conception of Red Right Hand is that it would not be able to sneak onto a small liberal arts college campus and actually blend in. They are theoretically highly trained and experienced soldiers, and would not match the crowd of a bunch of 18-21 year olds. (It also may not exist long-term in TWIE, seeing as how O5-1 designates Samsara as his RRH.) A shapeshifting Overseer, however, can take the appearance of a late teenager and fit perfectly, even mimicking a specific person.

I understand if you still don't like the content, but the majority of your concerns are because this is highly based on another story and are a result of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this considerably more interesting than roller derby. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How can the O5 Council be well-known enough that their track records and deaths are seemingly common knowledge? Why would one of them be willing to appear at a college speaking event? And why on earth would an Overseer personally try to sneak onto a college campus? Isn't that exactly the kind of thing that Red Right Hand is for? Every single one of these events is just an absurd breach of secrecy for the Foundation. I just cannot buy that any event in this string of interactions would ever take place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has Big Reed Energy. it's pretty much exactly the feel I was going for with Deer College, and I am beyond jazzed that someone else finally wrote one of my little GoI formats. +1.

item.title: Deer and the Foundation
item.content:encoded:
I've wanted to write one of these for a while.

The quote at the beginning is a parody of a quote about Reed, which I found in a Student Handbook.

Thanks to ch00bakka for looking this over before posting.
